channel.title: Health and Social Care Committee — News

item.title: Prime Minister will have ‘no excuse’ not to start fixing social care before general election, MPs say
item.description: The Health and Social Care Committee has urged the next Prime Minister to confirm

item.title: Amos maternity care report: Health Committee Chair comments
item.description: Health and Social Care Committee Chair, Layla Moran MP, said:

item.title: Government will improve children’s palliative care and 24/7 services in response to MPs’ report
item.description: The Government has promised to set legal minimum standards for children’s and young people’s palliative care.

item.title: SEND, mental health and prevention overlooked in NHS reforms, say MPs preparing amendments to Health Bill
item.description: The Health and Social Care Committee has today published a report outlining amendments to the Health Bill

item.title: Children and young people’s mental health: committees launch survey and announce first evidence session
item.description: The Education and Health and Social Care committees have launched a survey to capture the individual experiences of young adults who have received mental health services.

item.title: Physical activity as important as medication for keeping older people healthy and happy, MPs say
item.description: Physical activity should be at the heart of the NHS’s support for older people and is as important as providing medication, a report by the Health and Social Care Committee says.

item.title: Government refuses to set targets for health visitor recruitment or to develop new vaccinations strategy
item.description: The Government has ruled out developing a new national vaccinations strategy

item.title: IEP members announced for investigation into young people’s transition from child to adult health and social care services
item.description: The Health and Social Care Committee has announced new members that will join its Independent Expert Panel (IEP)

item.title: Children’s end of life services among areas that desperately need addressing in reforms to palliative care, says Health Committee
item.description: A new report by the Health and Social Care Committee challenges the Government to ensure its forthcoming

item.title: Chair criticises Govt’s ‘disappointing’ response to report on strengthening community mental health services
item.description: Health and Social Care Committee Chair, Layla Moran MP, gave an emotive statement in the House of Commons Chamber on 19 March

item.title: Independent Expert Panel to investigate pitfalls in young people’s transition to adult health and care services
item.description: The Health and Social Care Committee today announces that its Independent Expert Panel (IEP) will investigate to what extent young people are being let down when transitioning from children’s services to adult services in health and care.

item.title: Government accepts many conclusions of Independent Expert Panel’s report on palliative care services in England
item.description: The Government has published its response to a report about problems facing palliative and end of life care services in England, stating that it either accepts or partially accepts the report’s conclusions.

item.title: Committees to examine children and young people’s mental health in new inquiry
item.description: Today the Education and Health and Social Care committees have launched a new inquiry into children and young people’s mental health.

item.title: Cancer Plan: Health Committee will push Ministers for real change and patients’ voice to be heard
item.description: The Chair of the Health and Social Care Committee, Layla Moran MP, has reacted to the Government’s announcement on its National Cancer Plan for England. It comes as Ministers explained the Plan to MPs in the House of Commons chamber today.

item.title: Boost infants’ life chances with more Family Hubs, health visitors and new vaccines strategy, MPs tell Govt
item.description: The UK has some of the worst health outcomes for young children in Europe. But a new report by the Health and Social Care Committee says that improving access to health visitors and Family Hubs whilst boosting vaccine uptake would help improve the lives of infants in England.

item.title: NHS’s shift to neighbourhoods could flounder without strengthening the workforce, Health Committee tells Govt
item.description: The Health and Social Care Committee has warned that the Government’s 10 Year Workforce Plan for the NHS shifting services from hospitals to neighbourhoods.

item.title: Failings in black maternal health: Govt responds to Committee’s report
item.description: The Government has announced it will soon roll out a new system for understanding the reasons behind severe failures in maternity care, in its response to a report on Black Maternal Health by the Health and Social Care Committee. However the Government has rejected calls to require midwives to undertake cultural competency training.

item.title: Health Committee launches new inquiry into transforming NHS’s local estates
item.description: The Health and Social Care Committee will investigate what the Government needs to do to make the NHS estate fit for the future, as Ministers plan their vision for a neighbourhood health service.

item.title: Govt risks missing golden opportunity to transform mental health services, Health Committee says
item.description: A new Health and Social Care Committee report urges the Government not to miss a golden opportunity to dramatically improve England’s mental health services.

item.title: Committee publishes Independent Expert Panel’s report on palliative care services in England
item.description: The Independent Expert Panel (IEP), commissioned by the Health and Social Care Committee, has produced a report on England’s palliative and end of life care services.
